Doc Toolkit Router Skill
nodes.rationale: Routes image/document requests to one of three paths (TOOL/CODE/HUMAN) via a 3-question test; identity documents never go to web services regardless of route.

nodes.label: Passport Photo Skill
nodes.rationale: Infers spec from user words (never asks if inferable); surfaces warnings; human must still verify head position/expression/background.

nodes.label: Shrink File Skill
nodes.rationale: Two modes: lossy target-size (SSIM-guarded, PDFs only) for portal limits; lossless (bit-identical pixels, never larger) for pdf/jpg/png.

nodes.label: Gazelle Python Manifest
nodes.rationale: Generated import-to-pip-dist mapping (fitz->pymupdf, PIL->pillow); checked in so no Gazelle resolve directives are needed.

nodes.label: tools/cli.sh CLI Entrypoint
nodes.rationale: Self-bootstraps the venv so skills need zero setup; hermetic alternative is bazel run //:py_cli with absolute paths.

nodes.label: doc-toolkit MCP Server
nodes.rationale: 7 MCP tools exposing the same use cases as the CLI, with one deliberate exception: Ghostscript escalation is CLI-only so served paths stay AGPL-free.

nodes.label: Shared Kernel
nodes.rationale: Immutable value objects used by all contexts; W2 moves (ImageKind, ResampleFilter, CompressionTarget, TargetSizeSearch, LosslessOutcome, constants) ended the image<->pdf bidirectional coupling flagged in docs/REVIEW.md.

nodes.label: QualityFloor
nodes.rationale: A domain invariant — an adapter may not return a result below it. SSIM 0.90 shown decorative (RESEARCH §3); target is SSIMULACRA2 >= 70 portal / 80 archival; single enforcement point lands in W5.

nodes.label: TargetSizeSearch
nodes.rationale: Binary search over encoder quality (monotone size<->quality predicate), then dimensions if the floor conflicts with budget. One algorithm home — photo byte ceilings reuse it rather than reimplementing.

nodes.label: BudgetDecomposition
nodes.rationale: Allocate a PDF's byte budget across images proportional to rendered area — spend bytes where eyes go; reserve non-image bytes measured after the structural pass.

nodes.label: EffectiveDpi
nodes.rationale: Computed from page geometry (CTM), never image metadata — scanners often omit or lie. Hard rule #3.

nodes.label: PhotoSpec Presets
nodes.rationale: Compliance specs are data presets, never code branches — a new country is a new row. Presets: us_passport, india_passport, india_oci; W7 adds min_bytes and head-rule fields as data.

nodes.label: SheetLayoutService
nodes.rationale: Pure tiling math: integer capacity floor(usable/cell), slack distributed as n+1 equal gaps that become cut lanes; 2x2 inch on 4x6 at 300 DPI gives 6-up matching commercial strips.

nodes.label: LosslessOptimizer Port
nodes.rationale: Pixels must be bit-exact: JPEG is entropy-coding-only (mozjpeg, DCT coefficients untouched); PNG recompress. Hard rule #1 (never larger) applies.

nodes.label: Target-Size Compression Algorithm
nodes.rationale: Shared across languages: structural lossless pass, CTM census, budget allocation proportional to rendered area, per-image branch by ImageKind (bitonal -> CCITT G4; contone -> DPI cap + quality binary search), retry with lowered dpi_cap, Ghostscript escalation only on BudgetMissed.

nodes.label: Hard Rules (tests, not suggestions)
nodes.rationale: Six rules encoded as tests in every language: never emit larger output; bitonal via gray-downsample+Otsu+CCITT G4 (never NN, never JPEG); effective DPI from CTM; quality floor is a domain invariant; every adapter behind a port with AGPL deps isolated; frozen-dataclass type discipline mirrored as Go interfaces and Rust traits.

nodes.label: pikepdf Composite Engine (default)
nodes.rationale: Default because the hosted-UI roadmap makes AGPL section 13 network copyleft a real obligation: pikepdf+pypdfium2+Pillow serve with no source-disclosure requirement and measured higher SSIM than both AGPL engines. Surgical image-XObject rewrite; pypdfium2 is only the renderer for SSIM.

nodes.label: pymupdf+pillow Engine (AGPL)
nodes.rationale: Smallest files but AGPL — avoid for network-served use (section 13); slight generational loss.

nodes.label: Ghostscript Escalation Engine
nodes.rationale: Escalation only, fires on BudgetMissed; AGPL, quarantined; slowest with no size/quality edge (ANALYSIS verdict).

nodes.label: N2: No AGPL on Any Served Path
nodes.rationale: Enforced by an import-contract test, not convention — one breach shipped before the test existed. AGPL deps (PyMuPDF, MuPDF, Ghostscript) isolated to their adapter modules; license quarantine as an architecture is a stated differentiator.
nodes.norm_label: n2: no agpl on any served path

nodes.label: N1: Local-Only Privacy
nodes.rationale: Online tools leak identity documents; everything runs on this machine. Identity documents never go to web services under any route.

nodes.label: Ssimulacra2Meter Adapter (W6)
nodes.rationale: Subprocess ssimulacra2_rs or vendored py behind QualityMeter; score 0-100, floor 70 portal / 80 archival; compares codec-loss only at native resolution. SSIM meter stays as fast fallback.

nodes.label: JpegliCodec Adapter (W6)
nodes.rationale: Subprocess cjpegli with native --target_size (internal Butteraugli-distance search); binary search demotes to fallback path. No pip binding exists, hence subprocess behind ImageCodec.

nodes.label: CtmCensus Interpreter (W5)
nodes.rationale: Content-stream interpreter ported from ocrmypdf _contentstream.py (MPL-2.0, ~130 lines): per-Do DPI via hypot(a,b), Form-XObject recursion. Fixes hard rule #3 — the mediabox approximation collapses budget weights on multi-image pages.

nodes.label: BackgroundMatter Port (W7)
nodes.rationale: MODNet ONNX adapter (Apache-2.0), never RMBG-1.4 (non-commercial); alpha mask composed via existing flatten() — matting not segmentation because hair/glasses edges need per-pixel alpha.

nodes.label: YuNetFaceLocator (W7)
nodes.rationale: cv2.FaceDetectorYN with 5 landmarks: anchor from face center, roll from atan2(eye_dy, eye_dx); not-exactly-one-face yields a warning.

nodes.label: OCRmyPDF
nodes.rationale: 34k stars, MPL-2.0 — best mechanics to port: full CTM content-stream interpreter, census safety rails (SMask/JPX/CCITT-G3 guards), DeviceGray preservation, deflate_jpegs, best bitonal handling (JBIG2).

nodes.label: Stirling-PDF
nodes.rationale: 87k stars; 9-level whole-doc ladder with no perceptual floor; patterns to steal: image dedup-by-hash before compression, fileUploadLimit, per-tool timeouts.

nodes.label: HivisionIDPhotos
nodes.rationale: 21k stars, Apache-2.0; validates the photo feature set but not the algorithms — its KB targeting is a linear quality walk with no floor vs our O(log) search with floor and warning.

nodes.label: SSIMULACRA2 Metric
nodes.rationale: SRCC 0.890 vs MOS on CID22 — the upgrade: XYB color (catches chroma damage), multi-scale, 4-norm pooling (a smashed MRZ line can't be outvoted by clean background), human-anchored 0-100 scale; floor 70 portal / 80 archival.

nodes.label: jpegli Encoder
nodes.rationale: google/jpegli, BSD-3: native --target_size via internal Butteraugli-distance search, ~35% density gain at equal perceptual quality, libjpeg62 ABI; no pip package so a subprocess adapter is required.

nodes.label: MODNet Matting Model
nodes.rationale: Photographic ONNX model, 24.7 MB, Apache-2.0, under 1 s on CPU — chosen for F8 background replacement; RMBG-1.4 excluded (BRIA non-commercial), BiRefNet wants GPU.

nodes.label: YuNet Face Detector
nodes.rationale: cv2.FaceDetectorYN, 345 KB, Apache-2.0 — box plus 5 landmarks give crop anchor, roll angle, and head-height compliance with plain affine math.

nodes.label: SSIM 0.90 Floor Invalidation
nodes.rationale: The floor is broken four ways: calibration off ~10x (real floors sit at 0.999+, 0.90 passes nearly any q>=30); luma-only misses chroma damage; global mean pooling lets clean background outvote destroyed text; 1024x768 pre-resize low-pass-filters away glyph-edge ringing. Plus a bug: floor-violation fallback returned quality_range[0], the worst image.

nodes.label: P0: AGPL Quarantine Breach on Served Path
nodes.rationale: mcp_server/server.py:153 — served MCP tool inspect_pdf imported AGPL PyMuPdfInspector, violating N2. Fix: license-clean inspector (pikepdf/pypdfium2), PyMuPDF stays CLI-only, import-contract test so it can't recur. Fixed in PLAN Wave 1.
nodes.norm_label: p0: agpl quarantine breach on served path

nodes.label: Surface-Parity Fiction Theme
nodes.rationale: CLI is the real product; MCP/HTTP/docs claim parity they don't deliver (missing /inspect, no escalation, no strip_metadata) and nothing tested N2 — root cause behind the P0.

nodes.label: Engine Benchmark Verdict (pikepdf default)
nodes.rationale: On the 16-page OCI corpus, pikepdf has highest fidelity (~0.999 SSIM, because it embeds the exact recompressed JPEG the search chose, no generational re-encode), is fastest, and is license-clean; its only trade-off is slightly larger files, all still under target. Validates the surgical-composite-beats-gs strategy.

nodes.label: Zero-Directive Gazelle Structure
nodes.source_location: §How each Gazelle directive was designed away
nodes.rationale: Design principle: a mature module structure that needs zero Gazelle directives — every directive eliminated by structure (Python package at repo root, AGPL adapters in their own package, go.mod module mode, checked-in manifest). Root BUILD.bazel contains no gazelle directives.

nodes.label: Hexagonal Architecture (arrows point inward)
nodes.rationale: domain -> application -> ports -> infrastructure in every context; swap adapters, test math with zero I/O. ABC when adapters subscribe nominally, Protocol when infra must not import application.

links.source_location: modules_mapping generated from the pinned wheel set
